lessons.title: Hello Python
lessons.description: Print text and understand basic syntax.

questions.questionText: Which function displays output in Python?
questions.explanation: print() sends text to the console.

questions.questionText: Fill the blank to print Hello.
questions.explanation: Use print as the output function name.

questions.questionText: What does this output? print(2 + 3)
Arrange to print Welcome.
questions.explanation: Create variable first, then print it.

questions.questionText: Which quote style is valid for strings?
questions.explanation: Python accepts both single and double quotes.

questions.questionText: Fix the comparison bug in the condition.
questions.explanation: Single = assigns a value, while == compares values.

slides.title: Welcome to Hello Python
slides.body: Print text and understand basic syntax.

slides.title: Core Concept
slides.body: In this lesson, you will focus on syntax and understand how it works in practical code.

slides.body: Relating code to real life makes it easier to remember.

lessons.title: Variables

questions.questionText: Which is a valid variable assignment?

questions.explanation: Variable name goes on the left side.

questions.questionText: What is the result of x = 2; y = 3; print(x + y)?

questions.explanation: Snake case with letters/underscores is valid.
